Barren.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ffff00]Medium[-][/c]

A planet with large amounts of fissile elements deep in its mantle, causing runaway nuclear reactions.

BarrenMetal.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ff0000]Hard[-][/c]

[c][e0e020]NOTE:[-][/c]   [c]Multiple anti-aircraft turrets detected. [-][/c]
[c][808080][i]Use extreme caution.[-][/i][-][/c] 

During a planet's formation, most of the heavy elements sink down into the core. 
But if a cosmic accident strips a world of it's outer layers, or if there was abundant material - then there can be such a thing as a 'metal planet'. 

In any case, there will be a 'thin' silicate mantle left, of 'just a few hundred' kilometers. 
Still, there are vast quantities left to mine, and deposits of heavy and exotic elements are readily accessible, too. 

Since this is a rare place, it attracts many 'protectors'. Only the really careful or the most powerful will find a way to navigate... -Trespassers, beware.

Cothga.Description:
Travel serves more than war, trade and leisure. 

Because the more isolated a place is -  more the sooner than the later a humble life reaches that shore, settles down, and gets a chance to rock the old dominion:
 
Plant seeds as stowaways in the chinks of jackboots, the creatures of the ducts of container shuttles, or introduced as companions, or to hunt. 

- Whoever is unaccustomed to learn and change, will be swept aside.

Desert.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ffff00]Medium[-][/c]

Water is quite common in the universe, as life-enabling liquid, or at least as ice or vapor.
However, not every planet is gifted with water. Some worlds get scorched by their home star, or maybe their magnetic field is too weak to stem against the push of the stellar wind.

But even this place of glaring sunshine has managed to keep some spots here or there...

Difficulty: [c][ff0000]Hard[-][/c]

An completely alien planet, full of danger.

EdenAlien2.Description:
A world in deep in blacklight. 
(Wear lots of sunscreen.) 
 
The atmosphere contains gases that are only passable for ultraviolet rays. 
While those are invisible to the human eye, from surfaces those may still be reflected back as 'light' - Resulting in bleak-yet-colourful landscapes.

EdenAridMesa.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ffff00]Medium[-][/c]

A dry planet with outcroppings of rock and clay.

EdenASCDesert.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ff0000]Hard[-][/c]

A hot, rocky desert. Patches of Neodymium can be detected on the surface.

This planet is occupied by the ASC Pirates.

EdenASCJungle.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ff0000]Hard[-][/c]

A jungle world with thick forests, teeming with life.

The thick vegetation impedes scans, making your handheld or ship mounted detectors all but useless in the jungle. You will have to rely on visual or close range HUD identification for all but the largest of structures.

What treasures can be found hidden in the jungle?

This planet is occupied by the ASC Pirates.

EdenASCSwamp.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ffff00]Medium[-][/c]

Rich in water but poor in soils, which creates a fertile breeding ground for dangerous creatures.

EdenBarrenElder.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ff0000]Hard[-][/c]

Recon Element snuffed out. No Intel.

A cold dark hell, - this world is supremely inhospitable.  Prospectors find common elements, as well as Neodymium and the occasional Erestrum deposit, -but most often all they find is terror and death.

[c][808080][i]Originally created by Monroe.[/i][-][/c]

EdenDesertKharak.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ffff00]Medium[-][/c]

The Hiigarans have landed a colony here from their journey to the Centaurus Cluster, and called it 'New Kharak', based on some distant planet similar to this one. The Zirax have setup some pretty stern defenses here, if somewhat green, guarding the ship graveyards which serve as valuable salvage.

The Hiigarans tried to have peaceful contact, but has slowly devolved into violence, with much infighting to control the salvage here. Many ships still litter the surface, and most of them could be returned to service, with the right repairs...

[c][DAA520][i]Tied first place for the November 2019 Custom Planet Challenge.[/i][-][/c]
[c][808080][i]Originally created by Fenra369.[/i][-][/c]

EdenDesertPummeled.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ffff00]Medium[-][/c]

[c][ffffff]Water Discovered[-][/c]

Once barren, deep meteor strikes have brought unknown sources of water to the surface...and with it, life...

House Rados and Polaris compete for resources, but the Epsilon faction has recently moved in, increasing tensions...

[c][808080][i]Originally created by Monroe.[/i][-][/c]

Difficulty: [c][ffff00]Medium[-][/c]
Sensor Interference: [c][ffff00]POI detection range reduced.[-][/c]

EdenDesertWhite.Description:
Difficulty: [c][FF0000][b]Hard[/b][-][/c]

For the first few years the colony was successful by leaps and bounds, until it lost contact.  Investigators were sent but no information returned.  The planet was originally sought for its unique fauna and primitive life. Hope jumped that the system would serve as an ideal location as a bridgeway to further exploration into that galaxy.  Apparently, something else sought the location as well.

[c][DAA520][i]Tied first place for the November 2019 Custom Planet Challenge.[/i][-][/c]

Difficulty: [c][ffffff]Unknown[-][/c]

EdenFrozenInferno.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ffff00]Medium[-][/c]

Strong Rados Presence Detected
The Rados faction of the Zirax Empire has established multiple missle research facilities on this bleak frozen inferno.  Poor atmospheric conditions lead us to believe their goal is to perfect their missle accuracy.

Extinguish all research at any cost.

[c][808080][i]Originally created by Monroe.[/i][-][/c]

EdenGasDwarfBlue.Description:
[c][e0e020] NOTE: For safety reasons, your ship's computer will prevent you from entering the atmosphere. [-][/c] 
Not all gas planets are born giants: If not enough material was in the vicinity during their formation, some stay rather small. 
Nonetheless, this is a huge mass of Hydrogen, enveloping some small rocky core. Traces of other gases give the clouds some colour. 

Violent storms in the atmosphere can tear any daring craft apart - or the pressure on the 'surface' would crush them. 
- Landing is not advised.

[c][808080][i]Originally created by Needleship.[/i][-][/c]

Difficulty: [c][ffff00]Medium[-][/c]

A powerful blast of radiation sometime in the past incinerated half of this planet, causing the eventual collapse of a once thriving civilization. The few surviving lifeforms in the blast zone were severely mutated as a result.

Difficulty: [c][ff0000]Hard[-][/c]

The molten mantle still breaks through cracks in the thin crust on this young planet. Several houses of the Zirax Empire compete against each other to claim its vast resources for themselves, but aren't beyond cooperating with each other to fight off any would-be invaders.

Barely out of its infancy, debris still rain down on this planet. Meteors penetrate the thin atmosphere every few days, bringing valuable resources with them.

EdenLegacyHome.Description:
[c][e0e020][DANGER][-][/c]   [c]Containment Zone![-][/c]

Difficulty: [c][ff0000]***[-][/c][c][ff00ff][b] ULTRA [/b][-][/c][c][ff0000]***[-][/c]
[c][ffffff][i](Ultra difficulty presents extreme challenges and may result in loss of your ship with little warning)[-][/i][/c]

[c][ffff00]High Gravity Warning[-][/c]

Shrouded by a thick atmosphere and a surface that absorbs most light, this planet has been heated up to extreme temperatures. All visibility is lost on the planet's surface, but sensors indicate vast quantities of gold ore.

EdenMolten.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ff0000]Hard[-][/c]
[c][ffffff]Mining deposits on this planet requires use of an autominer device.[-][/c]
[c][ffffff]Magmacite formations detected.[-][/c]

This world is either in its earliest stages of development or suffered a recent cataclysm that left it almost completely molten.
Thin plates rise and solidify on the surface, bringing mineable metals both mundane and precious with them to the surface.

EdenMoonAlien.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ff0000]Hard[-][/c]

An alien world with strange organisms. Unknown substances ooze up from scars in the surface.

EdenMoonBarren.Description:
Difficulty: [c][00ff00]Easy[-][/c]

This barren rock carries its history on the surface:
Completely solidified, and no atmosphere to remove tracks and shapes -
All encounters will be remembered.

EdenMoonBarrenCobalt.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ffff00]Medium[-][/c] 

A Cobalt rich barren moon, devoid of any native life.

EdenMoonBarrenCopper.Description:
Difficulty: [c][00ff00]Easy[-][/c]

This copper rich moon has a very thin atmosphere - It is shielded by an unusually large magetic field of the nearby planet, and the moon is dense enough to keep some gases from drifting into space. By this it has accumulated extreme amounts of desert varnish.

EdenMoonBarrenGas.Description:
Difficulty: [c][00ff00]Easy[-][/c]

This moon's powerful magnetic field protects it from radiation but also interferes with computer systems.
CPU tier is restricted to T2.

Difficulty: [c][00ff00]Easy[-][/c]

An otherwise standard barren moon that has very large fields of iron ore in its craters.

EdenMoonBarrenSath.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ffff00]Medium[-][/c]

It is rare for a celestial body as small as this to have an atmosphere. Nevertheless, somehow this moon was able to hold on to one.

EdenMoonBarrenSilicon.Description:
Difficulty: [c][00ff00]Easy[-][/c]

Silicon rich sandy moons such as these often attract a strong civilian presence, lured either by trade, research, or industry.

EdenMoonCrystalErestrum.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ff0000]Hard[-][/c]

This moon is used by XCorp as a testing ground for new Drone AI and other technologies. The drones on this planet may behave in unpredictable ways.

Sometimes a barren moon forms with a highly crystallized crust. These so-called crystalline moons are highly coveted for the natural resources they contain or as places of spiritual significance. Autominers may be required to mine some of the Erestrum on this moon.

Difficulty: [c][00ff00]Easy[-][/c] 

An icy companion, with an ocean deep between its crust and rocky core. Faint organic substances leave colourful streaks on the surface. - A memory of a moon back in the Sol system...

EdenMoonIceOuter.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ff0000]Hard[-][/c] 

Deep ice rifts scar the frozen landscape of this icy moon. Deposits of Pentaxid have been detected within the soft layers of the ice.

Protected by a thick atmosphere and strong magnetic field, this moon is home to lush jungle and rugged mountaintops.

EdenMoonLava2.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ff0000]Hard[-][/c] 

Fissures, mountain ranges, and lakes of hot lava dominate the landscape.

Difficulty: [c][ffff00]Medium[-][/c]
[c][ffff00]High Gravity Warning[-][/c]

A dense iron core covered by an impenetrable layer of solidified metal. Hotspots of molten gold allow access to the softer mantle, providing a way of mining the gold deposits trapped beneath the crust.
Sensors detect Iron deposits that can be indefinitely mined via autominer without depleting. Platinum deposits can be mined the same way but will deplete.

EdenMoonMethane.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ffff00]Medium[-][/c] 

Methane oceans hide under the thick haze of this cold and desolate moon.

EdenMoonMethane2.Description:

Difficulty: [c][00FF00][b]Medium[/b][-][/c]

This moon was once the center of a terraforming attempt run by the local colony.  The plant likely ceased activity at the same time the planets colony lost contact.  The soon to be habitable moon, now slipping back to its former self.  The moons almost breathable atmosphere now nothing more than an unending cloud of Sulfur.

[c][DAA520][i]Tied first place for the November 2019 Custom Planet Challenge.[/i][-][/c]
[c][808080][i]Originally created by Ravis.[/i][-][/c]

EdenMoonTemperate.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ffff00]Medium[-][/c]

A temperate moon with wide open plains.

[c][808080][i]Originally created by Javarox87.[/i][-][/c]

EdenMoonTemperate2.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ffff00]Medium[-][/c]

EdenMoonToxic.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ff0000]Hard[-][/c]

Originally planned to be a mining moon, the idea was abandoned when miners broke into an alien nest and the mine was overrun. Now this highly radioactive moon is used as a dumping ground for toxic waste.

EdenMoonVolcanic.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ffff00]Medium[-][/c]

[c][ffff00]Tectonic Instability:[-][/c] [ffffff]Bases cannot be constructed on the surface.[-][/c]

A barren moon with a thin atmosphere and heavy volcanic activity fueled by the tidal forces of the nearby planet and sister satellites. Temperates can very considerably across the surface. Truly a world of fire and ice.

EdenOcean2.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ff0000]Hard[-][/c] 

Vast oceans cover the majority of the planet's surface. A high concentration of greenhouse gases in the atmosphere has kept the planet relatively warm, considering its distance from the sun.

[c][ffff00]High Gravity Warning[-][/c]

Sensors detect the presence of [c][ff00ff]Abyssal Zirax[-][/c]. Extreme caution is advised.

EdenStarterAridRE.Description:
[sup]Location: [c][A9A9A9][-][/c][c][[c][ffff00][b]Omicron[/b][-][/c][c]][-][/c] [c][C0C060]Roggery[-][/c]  [c][C0C060]Uai[-][/c]  [c][C0C060]Ningues[-][/c]                             [c][6F6F6F]04 of 17[-][/c]

Difficulty: [c][ffff00][b]Medium[/b][-][/c]                                      Start: [c][FFFFFF]Escape Pod[-][/c]
Starting Missions: [c][FFFFFF]Reforged Story[-][/c]             Equipment: [c][FFFFFF]Basic[-][/c]
[c][A9A9A9]- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -[-][/c]
Parched mountains, rugged savanna and grueling plains, shriveled lake-beds with the remains of once thriving plant and animal life,
this hot, dry and unrelenting arid planet has a thin atmosphere that
is un-breathable by humans. Oxygen must be generated from what few remaining lakes dot the baked landscape.
Be warned, choosing this planet as the survival start is
recommended for experienced players only.[/sup]

EdenStarterBeginner.Description:
Difficulty: [c][00ff00][b]Easy[/b][-][/c]
[c][00ffff][b]Beginner[/b][-][/c], Temperate, Temperate2
[c][FFFFFF]_______________________________________________[-][/c]

Missions: [c][FFFFFF]Default[-][/c]                 Solar System: [c][FFFFFF]Alpha[-][/c]
[c][00ffff]Cycle through available starting locations in Project Eden by left or right clicking on the [c][ffff00]Start Location[-][/c] [c][00ffff]button on the right window.[-][/c]
This is a very easy Temperate starting planet with no base attacks and increased resources for a more casual starting experience.

There are one way portals available near the crash site to 2 other beginner starting planets to prevent overcrowding on multiplayer servers.
[c][ffffff][i]Information on the scenario can be accessed in the Empyriopedia section of the PDA (F1).[-][/i][/c]

Haven is a very easy Temperate starting planet with no base attacks, few dangers, and increased resources. Good for new players or those who want a casual starting experience.[/sup]

EdenStarterDead.Description:
[sup]Location: [c][A9A9A9][-][/c][c][[c][FF0000][b]Purgatory[/b][-][/c][c]][-][/c] [c][C06060]Dread[-][/c]  [c][C06060]Rogue J-5565[-][/c]                             [c][6F6F6F]12 of 17[-][/c]

Difficulty: [c][FF0000][b]Very Hard[/b][-][/c]                                   Start: [c][FFFFFF]Cryo Facility[-][/c]
Starting Missions: [c][FFFFFF]Custom[-][/c]                         Equipment: [c][FFFFFF]Minimal[-][/c]
[c][A9A9A9]- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -[-][/c]
[c][ffff00][i]Blueprint factory disabled on this planet. Solar power ineffective.[/i][-][/c]
After a nuclear war devastated your planet, you were put into cryosleep to await transportation to an interplanetary Arkship. The ship never came. Years later, you awaken to find yourself on a dead planet in the midst of a nuclear winter.
This planet has been stripped of all natural resources and is almost completely devoid of any life.
You will need to brave the intense cold and deadly radiation while you scavenge and salvage for the supplies needed to survive and eventually build a small vessel capable of escaping your former home.[/sup]

[c][ffffff][i]Information on the scenario can be accessed in the Empyriopedia section of the PDA (F1).[-][/i][/c]

This ocean planet seems to have no ore deposits near your position. You will have to build a vehicle to get to them, by the scarce means you can find while surrounded by wildlife, and maybe hunted down by a patrol vessel.
[i]For experienced players. This can be a difficult start with very slow progression, depending on planet size and seed luck.[-][/i][/sup]

After being captured by the Zirax, your prison transport crash landed on a previously undiscovered rogue planet just outside the local solar system.
You must escape the prison transport, fight off Zirax patrols and hostile wildlife, and survive a brutal environment.[/sup]

EdenStarterRogueDark.Description:
[sup]Location:  [c][A9A9A9][-][/c][c][[c][FF00FF][b]Rogue PX-37[/b][-][/c][c]][-][/c]                                                           [c][6F6F6F]15 of 17[-][/c]
System:  [c][A9A9A9]Alpha[-][/c]  [c][A9A9A9]Beta[-][/c]  [c][A9A9A9]Delta[-][/c]  [c][A9A9A9]Epsilon[-][/c] [c][[c][FF00FF][b]Rogue[/b][-][/c][c]][-][/c]
Difficulty: [c][FF00FF][b]Ultra Hard[/b][-][/c]                                  Start: [c][FFFFFF]Crashed Ship[-][/c]

[c][ffff00][i]Blueprint factory disabled on this planet, its moons, and orbit.
No sunlight, no solar power, pitch black.[/i][-][/c]
After being captured by the Zirax, your prison transport crash landed on a previously undiscovered rogue planet outside any known star system.
You must escape the prison transport, fight off Zirax patrols and hostile wildlife, and survive a brutal pitch black environment.
This starting planet will push even the most diehard survivors to the limit.[/sup]

EdenStarterSnow.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ffff00][b]Medium[/b][-][/c]
Arid, Swamp, Odd Temperate, [c][ffff00][b]Snow[/b][-][/c]
[c][FFFFFF]_______________________________________________[-][/c]
Start: [c][FFFFFF]Escape Pod[-][/c]                 Equipment: [c][FFFFFF]Basic[-][/c]
Missions: [c][FFFFFF]Default[-][/c]                  Solar System: [c][FFFFFF]Beta[-][/c]
[c][ffff00][i]Patrol Vessel Detected[/i][-][/c]
[c][808080]Default Snow starting planet.[-][/c]
A world at the far edge of the habitable zone, deep in the throes of a major ice age. Watch out for hot tubs and warm areas around lakes and in lower altitudes. The atmosphere is breathable. 
A challenging start, intended for [c][FFFFFF]experienced survivalists[-][/c] with a passion for hot beverages. 
 
[i]- Instant freezing, use the heater. Night kills! 
- High risk of starvation; sparse resources.[/i]
[c][ffffff][i]Information on the scenario can be accessed in the Empyriopedia section of the PDA (F1).[-][/i][/c][/i]

EdenStarterSnowDwarf.Description:
[sup]Location:  [c][C0A060]Old Pandora[-][/c] [c][A9A9A9][-][/c][c][[c][FFA500][b]Old Sienna[/b][-][/c][c]][-][/c]                                        [c][6F6F6F]17 of 17[-][/c]

An uninhabited icy dwarf. Scattered hotsprings and forests provide a small buffer against the cold during the day, but shelter must be built quickly to survive the freeze of the first night. A few faint power signals can be detected on the surface, suggesting that you are not the first to visit this world.
The extreme environment and low number of ores provides a harsh challenge.[/sup]

HomeworldZiraxMoon.Description:
[c]Backwater world; meager resources. [-][/c] 
[i][c][808080][i](Calculating the [b]map markers[/b] by statistical probability. - Sorry, no guarantees given!)[/i][-][/c] 
 
[i]Once upon a time,[/i] ... the planets were still forming. Gaining mass and changing their paths around their star. 
 
Gravitational disturbances sweeped through the whole system, hurtling planetisimals everywhere. 
 
... This is a wanderer, born and expelled from the stellar asteroid belt. 
It got caught up by the nearby large planet. - Somehow avoiding to collide, or to be flung away. 
 
But during those narrow, chaotic first passes, it experienced massive tidal forces. - 
Kneading it like dough, close of ripping it apart. 
 
 
- Times are much calmer now. 
 
And with that, all those scars make for impressive vistas, and stories of adventure.

JaschaMemorial.Description:
This planet contains a single structure: a memorial to a beloved member of the Empyrion community.
This is a peaceful place for reflection.

Lava.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ff0000]Hard[-][/c]

Planets are born by the swirling, scattered ashes - the legacy of dead, exploded stars. 
...Gravity will collect all this messy debris, and give it a new form. 
 
But a world's amount of rock and dust, slowly grinding, clumping together... will result in an enormous amount of friction. 
 
Which means, [b][i]heat[/i][/b]. 
 
This place is just a mere quarter billion years old, and struggles to radiate all this excess heat into space - 
The bigger the planet, the longer it takes. 
 
In this liquid state, the planet resembles more a giant cooking pot, or lava lamp, or a blast furnance: 
Continents of less dense slag skim the surface, while the heavy, exotic metals sink into in planet's deep inner. 
 
Yet, by thermal churning and stirring, some bits of that precious core will be again swept back out, in reach of the surface. 
 
For those who brave these temperatures, there will be great rewards.

LavaNascent.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ff0000]Hard[-][/c]

[c][e0e020]WARNING:[-][/c]   [c][b]High gravity[/b]. Hostile.[-][/c] 
[c][808080][i]Don't get stranded down there.[-][/i][-][/c] 
 
A planet at the crossroads, shrouded in a thick cocktail of atmosphere: 
 
Will it become a boiling, or a frozen world? 
 
Or something [i]just[-][/i] in between? 
 
Will all the gases stay up in the sky, or condensate, to rain down? 
 
Will there be water? 
 
And how long will it last, after all? 
Will it stay as oceans and lakes, or drift into space? 
 
 
Even if this planet grows too hostile for life... that won't discourage the visitors. 
 
This is a young world. 
Expect rich ore deposits, a harsh climate, 
and laser shots from nowhere.

MoonDesert.Description:
Difficulty: [c][00ff00]Easy[-][/c]

This moon has a very thin atmosphere - It is shielded by an unusually large magetic field of the nearby planet, and the moon is dense enough to keep some gases from drifting into space. By this it has accumulated extreme amounts of desert varnish.

MoonForest.Description:
[c]Backwater world; meager resources. [-][/c] 
 
A small temperate moon, with unusual thick atmosphere - Even bristling with life! 
 
[i]So many things[/i] need to come together for such a world to occur: Water, a still fluid metal core, a decent size, in the right distance to the star... 
 
But as a superficial scan indicates, this moon is [i]resource poor[/i]. So there is not much interest in things that empires do - To claim, exploit, settle, or even just to visit. 
 
The drama of the universe continues elsewhere. While the canopies of the trees reach for sunlight. 
 
It is still a great spot if you want to get out of sight, or just to stretch your legs and wander.

MoonIce.Description:
Difficulty: [c][00ff00]Easy[-][/c]

An icy companion, with an ocean deep between its crust and rocky core. Faint organic substances leave colourful streaks on the surface. - A memory of a moon back in the Sol system...

Ocean.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ffff00]Medium[-][/c]

Travel serves more than war, trade and leisure. 

Because the more isolated a place is -  more the sooner than the later a humble life reaches that shore, settles down, and gets a chance to rock the old dominion:
 
Plant seeds as stowaways in the chinks of jackboots, the creatures of the ducts of container shuttles, or introduced as companions, or to hunt. 

- Whoever is unaccustomed to learn and change, will be swept aside.

SnowStory.Description:
Difficulty: [c][ffff00]Medium[-][/c]

Water is the great equalizer. 
 
Is it too hot? Then it will soak up the energy, to keep it for later. - Still liquid! 
Or too cold? - The water will move and spread the warmth around. 
 
And its ice will swim, and insulate the ocean below from freezing solid. 
By this, even a world of all-encompassing snow might change, evolve and thrive. 
 
Dare to exist near to a fickle, unpredictable star? 
Or far away, at the outer edges of its generosity? 
... Between hot and cold, with [i]water[/i] there is always a place for life!
